steps.title: Extract table values and compute differences
steps.content:
From the table:
  • $x = -1$: $f(x) = 16$
  • $x = 0$: $f(x) = 17$
  • $x = 1$: $f(x) = 18$
  • $x = 2$: $f(x) = 19$

The differences between consecutive outputs are:
  • $17 - 16 = 1$
  • $18 - 17 = 1$
  • $19 - 18 = 1$

steps.title: Identify the function type
steps.content: Since the differences are constant, the function is linear. Since $f(x)$ increases as $x$ increases (the differences are positive), the function is increasing linear. That matches choice B — B is correct.
